Built for places the internet forgot

Education shouldn't depend
on connectivity.

ỊMỤKÓYA is a low-tech, offline learning device designed to deliver full, curriculum-aligned education to children in underserved communities. It works without internet, without data, and without limitations.

The Challenge

Where we're starting and why it matters

When access is missing,
potential is lost

Millions of children are not out of school because they lack intelligence, but because education was never designed to reach them. No internet. No infrastructure. No teachers.
Just distance and delayed dreams.

Learning, redesigned
for reality

ỊMỤKÓYA brings the classroom directly to the learner. Preloaded with high-quality video lessons across 3 core subjects, it works completely offline and makes learning possible anywhere, anytime.

See how it works →

Everything a student needs.
Already inside.

From Primary to Senior Secondary School, every lesson is structured, aligned with the national curriculum, and designed for clarity and retention. No downloads. No subscriptions. No interruptions.

Built for communities,
not just classrooms

Whether in rural villages, homes, or underserved communities, ỊMỤKÓYA ensures that learning continues even when systems fall short.
